Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 12 Airlines That Still Operate 4-Engine Passenger Jets

Only three families of passenger quadjets remain in service around the world with a contracting number of commercial airlines.

The declining quadjet fleet reflects how modern twin-engine wide-bodies have become more economical for long-haul routes, forcing remaining operators to justify their 4-engine aircraft through ultra-premium capacity or niche markets.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 31, 2026

US asks Poland to send Patriot battery, missiles to Middle East

The United States has informally asked Poland to consider redeploying one of its two Patriot air defense batteries…...

U.S. defense requests for Poland's Patriot systems signal potential impacts on European air defense capabilities and aviation operations in contested airspace regions.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

China targets 140 launches in 2026 amid commercial space surge

China plans to conduct around 140 orbital launches this year, according to a commercial launch executive, marking a sharp acceleration in the country’s launch cadence.

China's 140 planned launches would exceed the entire global launch rate of recent years, signaling a dramatic shift in competitive advantage for satellite deployment and space infrastructure access.

See SpaceNews →

International Mar 31, 2026

Iran war forces IATA to cut global seat capacity growth projections

The International Air Transport Association (IATA) has said that global capacity growth eased to 3.3% year on year…...

Geopolitical tensions are now materially constraining airlines' fleet expansion plans, reducing expected capacity growth by approximately 1-2 percentage points from pre-conflict projections.

See AeroTime Hub →

PAI
Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

Operators must formally document their Safety Management System implementation to demonstrate FAA compliance, establishing a critical legal and operational accountability mechanism.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Massive 25-Hour Flights: Qantas' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Huge changes are coming to Qantas' longest services. See them all here!

Qantas' expansion into ultra-long routes will require significant fleet investments and crew scheduling innovations to maintain profitability on 25-hour operations.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

AI drones are now catching aerial threats like flying cops

XTEND is doubling down on one of the biggest challenges in modern warfare: how to stop hostile drones, without making things worse in the process. The AI-driven robotics company, backed by Eric Trump, has announced a new partnership with ParaZero Technologies as part of its broader $1.5 billion...

AI-enabled drone interception systems are emerging as critical military infrastructure, requiring partnerships between specialized robotics firms to balance threat elimination with collateral damage mitigation.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Two EA-37Bs Arrive at RAF Mildenhall on Possible First Operational Deployment

Two EA-37B Compass Call aircraft stopped at RAF Mildenhall for fuel during what may be the type’s first operational deployment abroad, reports suggesting Turkey as their destination. Two U.S. Air Force EA-37B Compass Call electronic warfare aircraft have arrived at RAF Mildenhall, UK, on Mar. 31,...

The EA-37B Compass Call's potential first operational deployment signals the U.S. Air Force's expanding electronic warfare capabilities in contested regions, marking a significant shift in regional defense posture.

See The Aviationist →

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Effective SMS depends less on formal procedures than on psychological safety that encourages frontline employees to report hazards without fear of retaliation.

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Europe’s space sector faces power shift as funding grows

New report from Aerospace Corp. highlights rising EU funding and defense focus...

Europe's increased space investment signals a strategic shift toward defense independence, creating new supplier opportunities for aerospace manufacturers across the continent.

See SpaceNews →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

DroneShield opens Amsterdam HQ as counter-drone demand grows

Drone threats aren’t slowing down in Europe, and neither is DroneShield. The Australia-based counter-drone and electronic warfare specialist has officially opened its new European headquarters in Amsterdam, signaling a deeper, more permanent push into one of its fastest-growing markets. more…...

DroneShield's European expansion reflects mounting commercial opportunity in counter-drone technology as regulatory frameworks struggle to contain proliferating aerial threats across the continent.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Embraer KC-390 Millennium showcases cold weather operations in Sweden

Embraer’s KC-390 Millennium demonstrator has completed short takeoff and landing operations at Sweden’s Vidsel Test Range as part of the…...

Cold-weather STOL validation in Nordic conditions strengthens the KC-390 Millennium's competitive positioning for high-latitude military and humanitarian logistics missions.

See AeroTime Hub →

General Aviation Mar 31, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Medium-hub airports like CID that balance flight training operations with commercial air carrier traffic face unique operational challenges requiring flexible infrastructure and scheduling protocols.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Sphere ditches contractors, speeds up HubT drone deployments

Sphere is tightening its grip on autonomous drone deployments with a set of practical upgrades to its HubT platform, but the biggest shift is how the system is now built. more…...

Sphere's transition to in-house drone operations reduces deployment timelines and operational complexity by eliminating contractor dependencies in autonomous delivery networks.

See DroneDJ →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Researchers say SAF made from solar energy could be scaled to industrial levels

The University of Sheffield has developed a new way of making sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) using solar energy…...

Solar-powered SAF production could reduce aviation's dependence on fossil fuels and grid electricity while scaling manufacturing capacity to meet growing decarbonization demands.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

UK drone rules just got way easier to understand

Grey Arrows Drone Club just did something UK drone pilots have quietly needed for years. And if you’ve ever second-guessed where you’re allowed to take off, this could be a game-changer. more…...

Simplified UK drone guidance reduces operator confusion and compliance errors, potentially lowering regulatory violations and insurance claims across the recreational sector.

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit design at busy airports like LAX creates systematic incursion risks that require pilots to execute precise post-landing procedures under high-workload conditions.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 5 Airlines With The Largest Boeing 737 Fleets Today

Boeing's single-aisle narrowbody family has been a popular choice for many years.

The 737's dominance across major carriers creates significant operational and supply chain dependencies that amplify the impact of Boeing's production and safety issues.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Rheinmetall and Boeing Australia formalize MQ-28 Ghost Bat bid

Germany’s interest in acquiring the MQ-28 Ghost Bat has moved from diplomatic signals to a formal industrial partnership in a…...

Germany's formal partnership with Boeing Australia signals competitive pressure on European unmanned systems programs and reshapes NATO's autonomous aircraft procurement landscape.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

MiniV-Bat 234g, 74 minutes hover bicopter

Bicopters are theoretically the most efficient multi-rotor design for their size constraint, yet their real-world...

Bicopter efficiency gains could reduce operational costs and expand mission range for small UAS operators facing weight and endurance constraints.

See sUAS News →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational efficiency improvements across all aviation system layers offer nearer-term emissions reductions than waiting for new aircraft technologies to scale industry-wide.

See MDPI →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

2026 might be the most affordable year to buy a DJI drone

DJI drones that once felt like serious investments are now showing up at prices that would’ve seemed unrealistic even a year ago. Thanks to the Amazon Big Spring Sale, models across the lineup are seeing steep discounts, opening the door at just $149 and making it very easy to imagine how different...

DJI's aggressive pricing strategy through major retailer partnerships is fundamentally shifting consumer drone adoption from hobbyist luxury to mass-market commodity.

See DroneDJ →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Drone survey without GCPs? Wingtra claims sub-3 cm accuracy

Wingtra is stepping into 2026 with a clear message for the surveying world: the future of mapping might not need ground control points at all. The Switzerland-based drone company recently launched the SURVEY61 payload, designed to work with the WingtraRAY drone. And the pitch is hard to ignore —...

Wingtra's elimination of ground control points through advanced sensor fusion could reduce surveying project timelines and costs by removing a major operational bottleneck.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Sikorsky Collaborates with Robinson Helicopter Company to Integrate MATRIX™ Autonomy into Robinson R66 TURBINETRUCK

Sikorsky, a Lockheed Martin company (NYSE:LMT), and Robinson Helicopter Company (RHC) unveiled the R66 TURBINETRUCK, a game-changing...

Sikorsky's MATRIX autonomy integration into Robinson's R66 expands autonomous capabilities beyond military platforms into the commercial light helicopter market, potentially accelerating adoption of autonomous systems in civil aviation operations.

See sUAS News →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Artemis 2 countdown underway

The two-day countdown for the Artemis 2 mission around the moon started March 30 with NASA officials reporting no major issues for the launch.

NASA's Artemis 2 readiness demonstrates sustained progress in lunar exploration infrastructure critical for aerospace industry supply chain and long-duration spaceflight capabilities.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 31, 2026

ICE Eyes 10 New Boeing 737 And Gulfstream G650s For Deportation Fleet

The controversial agency is jet shopping again.

ICE's expansion of its deportation fleet signals increased demand for used business and commercial aircraft, potentially affecting availability in secondary markets.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Inside The Boeing 747's Upper Deck Layout

From a secluded cocktail bar to a high-density seating zone, the evolution of the 747's upper deck reflects the changing priorities of aviation.

The 747's upper deck evolved from luxury amenities to revenue-maximizing high-density seating, mirroring airlines' shift from premium experience to capacity optimization.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Fires Tear Through Nebraska Grasslands

Dry, warm, and windy conditions across the U.S. Great Plains led to extreme fire activity in March 2026.

Extreme wildfire conditions in the Great Plains may impact regional airspace operations through smoke, visibility restrictions, and potential aircraft diversion requirements.

Military Mar 31, 2026

5 Fighter Jets With The Highest Top Speeds In Service Today

From the Mach 2.83 MiG-31 to the F-22, these are the 5 fastest fighter jets still in service—and why speed still matters in modern air combat.

Speed remains tactically relevant despite modern air combat emphasis on sensors and missiles, as supersonic capability provides crucial engagement advantages and escape options.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

JetBlue Hikes Checked Bags Fees, Cites "Rising Operating Costs"

JetBlue announces a hike checked bag fees, balancing the need to keep fares competitive with the reality of escalating operations costs.

JetBlue's fee increase signals that carriers are shifting cost pressures to ancillary revenue rather than base fares, a strategic response to margin compression in competitive markets.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

5 Routes That Push Aircraft To Their Range Limits

These services offer extra-long-range connectivity.

Ultra-long-range routes like Singapore-New York require aircraft operating at 95%+ of maximum range, leaving minimal fuel reserves and demanding precision fuel planning.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Second Starlink satellite suffers anomaly, generating debris

For the second time in just over three months, a SpaceX Starlink satellite has generated debris from an apparent on-orbit malfunction.

Repeated Starlink anomalies raise concerns about debris accumulation in low Earth orbit, potentially accelerating collision risks for operational satellites and the International Space Station.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Delta Slashes Domestic Boeing 767-400 Flights By 67% In Massive Schedule Shakeup

The airline will redeploy a significant portion of its 767-400ER fleet from domestic routes to services to and from Europe, affecting several routes.

Delta's shift signals that wide-body aircraft are becoming more economically viable on transatlantic routes than domestic markets, reflecting post-pandemic demand patterns and fuel efficiency gains.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Inside The F-35's Cockpit System

At the controls of flying supercomputer.

The F-35's integrated cockpit architecture consolidates multiple sensor feeds into a single pilot interface, fundamentally reducing cognitive load compared to legacy fighter platforms.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Rocket Lab wins German approval for Mynaric deal

Decision clears path for April closing after months of scrutiny over keeping key space technology in domestic hands...

German regulatory approval removes a critical hurdle for Rocket Lab's acquisition of Mynaric, enabling the consolidation of satellite communication capabilities within a U.S.-allied aerospace ecosystem.

See SpaceNews →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Video: Delta A330 returns to São Paulo after engine failure on takeoff

A Delta Air Lines Airbus A330-300 returned to São Paulo Guarulhos Airport shortly after takeoff on March 29,…...

Engine failures during takeoff remain among aviation's most critical phases, testing crew training and aircraft redundancy systems in real-world conditions.
